docs.text: Welcome to the Zephyr Documentation One Product. Three Editions. Everything You Need in One Place. We’ve recently consolidated Zephyr Squad and Zephyr Scale into a single product family: Zephyr . Product Overview Zephyr is SmartBear’s native test management solution for Jira. It helps teams plan, create, execute, and track their software testing activities directly within their Jira spaces. The platform centralizes test assets and provides a unified workspace where testers, developers, and product teams can collaborate effectively. Zephyr supports both manual and automated testing and streamlines QA workflows with built-in intelligent insights. It also includes AI capabilities that accelerate test creation, improve test coverage, and enhance overall efficiency. With clear visibility into test quality and release readiness, and with its close integration into work items and development processes, Zephyr allows teams to manage the complete testing lifecycle without switching tools. Get Started This section contains essential reference links required to get started with Zephyr: Enable Zephyr in your Jira Create your first test case Run your first execution Import tests Run automated tests with Zephyr Use reports and dashboards Key Concepts A brief introduction to essential components within Zephyr: Test Cases : Define the steps, data, and expected results for validating functionality Test Cycles : Organize and group tests for execution within a release or sprint Test Plans : Combine multiple cycles to manage testing across larger releases Automation : Integrate CI/CD pipelines, automation frameworks, and test results Integrations : Connect with tools like Jenkins, Bamboo, Cypress, JUnit, and more Resources Explore more learning and support materials: Video Tutorials Webinars Release Notes Community Discussion SmartBear Support SmartBear Academy View the latest product news in the Beamer newsfeed

docs.text: Zephyr Editions Comparison This page provides a side-by-side comparison of features available across different Zephyr editions. Whether you are evaluating which edition best fits your team’s needs or want to understand what is included in your current plan, this table helps you quickly compare the differences. Feature Zephyr Essential Zephyr Zephyr Advanced Manual test case management and execution ✔ ✔ ✔ Cross-space reporting and traceability ✔ ✔ ✔ Integration with automation frameworks (Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, Cucumber) and CI/CD ✔ ✔ ✔ BDD support ✔ ✔ ✔ REST API ✔ ✔ ✔ Data Residency ✔ ✔ Automatic test case generation using record and play (Web apps) ✔ ✔ No-code test automation (Web apps and APIs) ✔ ✔ Smart test-step suggestions ✔ ✔ Cross-browser testing ✔ (Chrome) ✔ (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari) Parallel testing ✔ (3 Parallels) SMS testing ✔ Email testing ✔ Notes Zephyr Essential provides core testing capabilities along with advanced automation features like no-code testing and parallel execution. Zephyr expands support for cross-browser testing and maintains strong integration capabilities. Zephyr Advanced focuses on enterprise-level needs such as scalability and broader traceability.
docs.title: Zephyr Editions Feature Comparison

docs.text: Zephyr Essential provides core testing capabilities along with advanced automation features like no-code testing and parallel execution. Zephyr expands support for cross-browser testing and maintains strong integration capabilities. Zephyr Advanced focuses on enterprise-level needs such as scalability and broader traceability.
docs.title: Notes

docs.text: Our roadmap Visibility of our roadmap Zephyr follows an agile development approach. The team continuously reviews progress, learns from outcomes, and adapts based on insights. Customer feedback plays a key role in shaping improvements and enhancing the overall product experience. The public roadmap provides visibility into the key features planned for the next 12 to 18 months. Disclaimer This roadmap includes information about upcoming features. The information is for reference only and should not be used for purchasing or planning decisions. All roadmap items are subject to change or delay. Development, release, and timing depend on evolving priorities and requirements. Feature requests and ideas Submit ideas to help improve Zephyr. Feedback helps prioritize features and identify valuable enhancements. Note Submitted ideas are not visible immediately. The product management team reviews each submission and evaluates it against existing ideas. The team publishes only ideas that meet priority and feasibility criteria.
docs.title: Roadmap
